zh.Cotton, Suffolk, England: 英國英格蘭素福爾可科頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Spencer於大約1537年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，她的父親Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire33歲，母親Lady Agnes Ann Spencer29歲。她於1558年與Lawrence Greene在英國倫敦結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1558年在英國英格蘭享年22歲。

zh-hans.Cotton, Suffolk, England: 英国英格兰素福爾可科頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Spencer于大约1537年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，她的父亲Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire33岁，母亲Lady Agnes Ann Spencer29岁。她于1558年与Lawrence Greene在英国倫敦结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1558年在英国英格兰享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Spencer was born about 1537, in London, Middlesex, England, her father, Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, was 33 and her mother, Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, was 29. She married Lawrence Greene in 1558, in London, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1558, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Spencer est née environ 1537, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Lawrence Greene en 1558, à Londres, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1558, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Spencer um 1537 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire 33 und ihre Mutter Lady Agnes Ann Spencer 29. Sie hat 1558 in London, England Lawrence Greene geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1558 im Alter von 22 Jahren in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Spencer è nata il circa 1537 nel London, Middlesex, England, sua padre, Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Lawrence Greene nel 1558, nel London, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1558, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 22 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Spencer(이)가 대략 1537년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes Ann Spencer(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1558년 영국 런던에서 Lawrence Greene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1558년 영국 잉글랜드에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Spencer nasceu aproximadamente 1537, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com Lawrence Greene em 1558, em London, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1558, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 22 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Spencer родилась около 1537 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lawrence Greene в 1558 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1558 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 22 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Spencer nació aproximadamente 1537, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Baron John Edward II de Spencer-van Stofold, Bedfordshire, Baron Spencer of Stofold, Bedfordshire, MP, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes Ann Spencer, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Lawrence Greene en 1558, en London, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió en 1558, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 22 años.
